segments.text: This is what the LORD says:
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text:
Behold, I will stir up against Babylon and against the people of Leb-kamai
the spirit of a destroyer.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: I will send strangers to Babylonto winnow her and empty her land; for they will come against her from every sidein her day of disaster.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Do not let the archer bend his bowor put on his armor. Do not spare her young men; devote all her army to destruction!
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: And they will fall slain in the land of the Chaldeans,  and pierced through in her streets.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: For Israel and Judah have not been abandonedby their God, the LORD of Hosts, though their land is full of guiltbefore the Holy One of Israel.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Flee from Babylon! Escape with your lives! Do not be destroyed in her punishment. For this is the time of the LORD’s vengeance; He will pay her what she deserves.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Babylon was a gold cup in the hand of the LORD, making the whole earth drunk. The nations drank her wine; therefore the nations have gone mad.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text:
Suddenly Babylon has fallen and been shattered. Wail for her; get her balm for her pain;
perhaps she can be healed.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: We tried to heal Babylon, but she could not be healed. Abandon her! Let each of us go to his own land, for her judgment extends to the sky and reaches to the clouds.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text:
The LORD has brought forth our vindication; come, let us tell in Zion
what the LORD our God has accomplished.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Sharpen the arrows! Fill the quivers!  The LORD has aroused the spiritof the kings of the Medes, because His plan is aimed at Babylonto destroy her, for it is the vengeance of the LORD—vengeance for His temple.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Raise a banner against the walls of Babylon; post the guard; station the watchmen; prepare the ambush. For the LORD has both devised and accomplishedwhat He spoke against the people of Babylon.
You who dwell by many waters, rich in treasures, your end has come; the thread of your life is cut.
The LORD of Hosts has sworn by Himself:
Surely I will fill you up with men as with locusts, and they will shout in triumph over you.
segments.speaker: the Lord

text: Praise to the God of Jacob

segments.text:
The LORD made the earth by His power; He established the world by His wisdom
and stretched out the heavens by His understanding.
When He thunders, the waters in the heavens roar; He causes the clouds to risefrom the ends of the earth. He generates the lightning with the rain and brings forth the wind from His storehouses.
Every man is senseless and devoid of knowledge; every goldsmith is put to shame by his idols. For his molten images are a fraud, and there is no breath in them.
They are worthless, a work to be mocked. In the time of their punishment they will perish.
The Portion of Jacob is not like these, for He is the Maker of all things, and of the tribe of His inheritance—the LORD of Hosts is His name.
You are My war club, My weapon for battle. With you I shatter nations; with you I bring kingdoms to ruin.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: With you I shatter the horse and rider; with you I shatter the chariot and driver.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text:
With you I shatter man and woman; with you I shatter the old man and the youth; 
with you I shatter the young man and the maiden.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text:
With you I shatter the shepherd and his flock; with you I shatter the farmer and his oxen; 
with you I shatter the governors and officials.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text:
Before your very eyes I will repayBabylon and all the dwellers of Chaldea
for all the evil they have done in Zion,
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: declares the LORD.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text:
Behold, I am against you, O destroying mountain, you who devastate the whole earth, declares the LORD. I will stretch out My hand against you; I will roll you over the cliffs
and turn you into a charred mountain.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text:
No one shall retrieve from you a cornerstoneor a foundation stone, 
because you will become desolate forever,
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: declares the LORD.

Raise a banner in the land! Blow the ram’s horn among the nations! Prepare the nations against her. Summon the kingdoms against her—
Ararat, Minni, and Ashkenaz. Appoint a captain against her; bring up horses like swarming locusts.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: Prepare the nations for battle against her—the kings of the Medes, their governors and all their officials, and all the lands they rule.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: The earth quakes and writhesbecause the LORD’s intentions against Babylon st and: to make the land of Babylon a desolation, without inhabitant.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: The warriors of Babylon have stopped fighting; they sit in their strongholds. Their strength is exhausted; they have become like women. Babylon’s homes have been set ablaze, the bars of her gates are broken.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: One courier races to meet another, and messenger follows messenger, to announce to the king of Babylonthat his city has been captured from end to end.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text:
The fords have been seized, the marshes set on fire, 
and the soldiers are terrified.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: For this is what the LORD of Hosts, the God of Israel, says:
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: The Daughter of Babylon is like a threshing floorat the time it is trampled. In just a little whileher harvest time will come.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon has devoured me; he has crushed me. He has set me aside like an empty vessel; he has swallowed me like a monster; he filled his belly with my delicacies and vomited me out.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: May the violence done to me and to my fleshbe upon Babylon,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: says the dweller of Zion.
May my blood be on the dwellers of Chaldea,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: says Jerusalem.
Therefore this is what the LORD says:
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Behold, I will plead your case and take vengeance on your behalf; I will dry up her sea and make her springs run dry.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Babylon will become a heap of rubble, a haunt for jackals,  an object of horror and scorn, without inhabitant.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: They will roar together like young lions; they will growl like lion cubs.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: While they are flushed with heat, I will serve them a feast, and I will make them drunkso that they may revel; then they will fall asleep forever and never wake up, declares the LORD.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: I will bring them down like lambs to the slaughter, like rams with male goats.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: How Sheshach has been captured! The praise of all the earth has been seized. What a horror Babylon has becomeamong the nations!
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: The sea has come up over Babylon; she is covered in turbulent waves.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Her cities have become a desolation, a dry and arid land, a land where no one lives, where no son of man passes through.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: I will punish Bel in Babylon. I will make him spew out what he swallowed. The nations will no longer stream to him; even the wall of Babylon will fall.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text:
Come out of her, My people!  Save your lives, each of you, 
from the fierce anger of the LORD.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text:
Do not let your heart grow faint, and do not be afraid
when the rumor is heard in the land; for a rumor will come one year—and then another the next year—of violence in the land and of ruler against ruler.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Therefore, behold, the days are comingwhen I will punish the idols of Babylon. Her entire land will suffer shame, and all her slain will lie fallen within her.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Then heaven and earth and all that is in themwill shout for joy over Babylonbecause the destroyers from the northwill come against her,
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: declares the LORD.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Babylon must fallon account of the slain of Israel, just as the slain of all the earthhave fallen because of Babylon.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: You who have escaped the sword, depart and do not linger! Remember the LORD from far away, and let Jerusalem come to mind.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: We are ashamed because we have heard reproach; disgrace has covered our faces, because foreigners have enteredthe holy places of the LORD’s house.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Therefore, behold, the days are coming,
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: declares the LORD,
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: when I will punish her idols, and throughout her land the wounded will groan.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Even if Babylon ascends to the heavens and fortifies her lofty stronghold, the destroyers I send will come against her,
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: declares the LORD.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: The sound of a crycomes from Babylon, the sound of great destructionfrom the land of the Chaldeans!
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: For the LORD will destroy Babylon; He will silence her mighty voice. The waves will roar like great waters; the tumult of their voices will resound.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: For a destroyer is coming against her—against Babylon. Her warriors will be captured, and their bows will be broken, for the LORD is a God of retribution; He will repay in full.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: I will make her princes and wise men drunk, along with her governors, officials, and warriors. Then they will fall asleep forever and not wake up,
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: declares the King, whose name is the LORD of Hosts.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: This is what the LORD of Hosts says:
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Babylon’s thick walls will be leveled, and her high gates consumed by fire. So the labor of the people will be for nothing; the nations will exhaust themselves to fuel the flames.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: This is the message that Jeremiah the prophet gave to the quartermaster Seraiah son of Neriah, the son of Mahseiah, when he went to Babylon with King Zedekiah of Judah in the fourth year of Zedekiah’s reign.
Jeremiah had written on a single scroll about all the disaster that would come upon Babylon—all these words that had been written concerning Babylon.
And Jeremiah said to Seraiah,
When you get to Babylon, see that you read all these words aloud,
segments.speaker: Jeremiah

segments.text: and say, ‘O LORD, You have promised to cut off this place so that no one will remain—neither man nor beast. Indeed, it will be desolate forever.’
segments.speaker: Jeremiah

segments.text: When you finish reading this scroll, tie a stone to it and cast it into the Euphrates.
segments.speaker: Jeremiah

segments.text: Then you are to say, ‘In the same way Babylon will sink and never rise again, because of the disaster I will bring upon her. And her people will grow weary.’
segments.speaker: Jeremiah

segments.text: Here end the words of Jeremiah.
